stemHtml: A pilot is flying manually, with the flight directors engaged, on the flight planned route towards a VOR that is 120 NM away from the current position. ATC then gives the pilot a right hand turn onto an assigned heading, shortening the route to only 90 NM. The pilot monitoring (PM) performs their actions, what do you need to do as a pilot flying (PF)?

options.a: You should initiate a right-hand turn as instructed by the ATC; the flight directors will catch up by the turn.
options.b: You should check the FMA and if the desired modes are not confirmed, ask the pilot monitoring to select the correct heading/track, and cross-check the selection again.
options.c: Order the pilot monitoring to reset the flight directors as they will automatically start the right turn after you initiate it.
options.d: Considering the current manual flying conditions, you shouldn’t follow the flight director orders.
